Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Employment Insurance Act (illness, injury or quarantine)
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Employment Insurance Act (illness, injury or quarantine)

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Employment Insurance Act to increase from 15 to 52 the maximum number of weeks for which benefits may be paid because of illness, injury or quarantine.
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: Paragraph 12(3)(c) of the Employment Insurance Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: because of a prescribed illness, injury or quarantine is 52;

Section.Text: Paragraph 152.14(1)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: because of a prescribed illness, injury or quarantine is 52;

Subsection.Text: In this section, other Act means the Budget Implementation Act, 2021, No. 1.
If section 1 of this Act comes into force before subsection 307(2) of the other Act, then that subsection 307(2) and section 323 of the other Act are repealed.
If subsection 307(2) of the other Act comes into force on the same day as section 1 of this Act, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that subsection 307(2) is deemed to have come into force before that section 1; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: section 323 of the other Act is deemed to have come into force before section 2 of this Act.
